name: plan_trip
description: Extract structured trip context from a natural language travel query. Use this to convert a user's raw request into the 9-dimension planning model (destination, dates, duration, travelers, budget, interests, accommodation, transport, constraints). Returns the extracted trip context along with planning progress (0-100%), current stage (discover/develop/refine/confirm), any detected conflicts, and a recommended next question to advance planning. Do not use this for exporting itineraries to maps — use export_gmaps instead.

query.description: Natural language trip description. Include destination, dates, number of travelers, budget tier, and interests when possible. Examples: '2 weeks in Japan for a couple, mid-range budget, interested in food and temples' or 'weekend getaway to Paris, solo, luxury'.

name: export_gmaps
description: Generate shareable Google Maps URLs from a saved itinerary. Use this when you have a completed itinerary with daily activities and locations. Takes an itinerary key from saved state and produces a full trip route URL plus per-day route URLs. Optionally exports a KML file for Google Earth or Google Maps import. Do not use this to generate itineraries — use plan_trip first.

itinerary_file.description: Key name of a saved itinerary in state (not a file path). The itinerary must have been saved with a 'days' array, where each day contains a 'day' number and a 'locations' array with objects having 'name', 'lat', and 'lng' fields.

export_kml.description: If true, also generate a KML file for import into Google Earth or Google Maps. Default is false.
